channel.title: Production on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Production on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: DSP 60 Science Per Minute Blueprint (v0.10) - Full Production Ratios From Raw Materials to All Science
item.description:
Short answer: 60 SPM (all six science types) requires: 120 Iron Ore/min, 60 Copper Ore/min, 30 Coal/min, 30 Crude Oil/min, plus off-world Titanium and Silicon.

Iron ore

Copper ore
Total about 30 assemblers, 40 smelters, and 15 chemical plants. The single most important ratio is 3 Blue : 3 Red : 6 Yellow : 8 Purple : 12 Green : 15 White assemblers.

Science production is measured in science per minute (SPM). 60 SPM is the standard mid-game target - achievable on a single planet, enough to research most technologies at a reasonable pace. This guide covers every production chain from raw materials to completed science matrices.

item.title: DSP Late-Game Factory Expansion Guide (v0.10) - Scale From 30 to 300 Science Per Minute
item.description:
Short answer: Do not build everything on one planet. Designate each planet for one purpose: smelting, component production, or science. Run Mk.III belts between clustered assembler groups. Use ILS for cross-planet transport of intermediate products. The bottleneck is always either Iron Ore throughput or Hydrogen supply - build 3x more of both than you think you need.

Scaling from 30 SPM to 300 SPM is not about building bigger factories. It is about building different factories. The single-planet approach breaks down around 60 SPM. Beyond that, you need a distributed multi-planet production network.

item.title: Build a Mall in 30 Minutes - Never Hand-Craft a Building Again (DSP Guide)
item.description:
Hand-crafting buildings is the #1 time waste in DSP. You spend 20 minutes crafting assemblers, belts, and sorters when you should be designing production lines.

Here is how to build a mall - a factory that produces every building automatically, so you never craft by hand again.

TL;DR: The Mall Concept

A mall is a production line where raw materials go in one end and every building type comes out the other. Each building type (belts, sorters, assemblers, furnaces, etc.) has one dedicated assembler with its own storage box. You walk by and grab a full stack of whatever you need.

item.title: DSP Automation Guide (v0.10) - Build Your First Automated Factory in 10 Minutes
item.description:
Dyson Sphere Program does not hold your hand. Your first hour can feel overwhelming. Dozens of buildings, multiple resources, and no clear starting point.

Here is the exact 5-step path to get your first automated factory running.

Your First Factory: 5-Step Setup

Step 1: Place Your First Miner

Find iron ore on your starting planet. Open the build menu, select the Mining Machine. Place it directly on an iron ore patch. Connect it to power with a Wind Turbine.

item.title: DSP Blueprint Guide (v0.10) - Copy and Paste Entire Factories in Seconds
item.description:
DSP v0.10 added a proper blueprint system. No more hand-placing every smelter. Here is everything you need to know about blueprints - from basic copy-paste to advanced mirroring and sharing.

TL;DR: How Blueprints Work in DSP v0.10

Press B to open the Blueprint Library. Select buildings (hold shift to multi-select), press CTRL+C to copy, then CTRL+V to paste. That is the basic flow. This guide covers the details that are not in the game.

item.title: DSP Smelting Array Blueprint Guide (v0.10) - 3 Smelters Feed a Mk.III Belt Perfectly
item.description:
Smelting is where every DSP run starts, and where most players first feel the pain of scaling up. One smelter looks fine. Ten smelters look decent. Fifty smelters feeding a sprawling factory reveal every bottleneck you buried under "it should work."

This guide covers smelting from the first furnace on a new planet through to full production columns — the correct ratios, the belt speeds that matter, and the layouts that scale cleanly without requiring you to redesign everything at every tier.

item.title: DSP Quantum Chips & Proliferator: Advanced Production Guide
item.description:
Every factory has a ceiling. At some point you're running 40 assemblers on your Circuit Board chain and wondering if there's a better way. In DSP v0.10, there is: Proliferator.

It's a resource you produce, spray onto your production lines, and watch your output climb without adding a single new assembler.

This guide covers the Quantum Chip production chain and explains the Proliferator system — what it is, how to produce it, what it does, and how to use it without turning your factory into a confusing mess of spray towers.
